No sampler / pruner changes. n_trials = 50 (up from 40; narrower space
gets finer coverage).

## 3. Rationale

Round 01 was a wide exploratory sweep. Two categorical axes
(`rerank_model`, `embed_model`) account for 0.68 of total importance, and
the top-6-trial cluster is unanimous on `(large, 3-large)`. Freezing both
collapses the study from 6 axes to 4 continuous/integer axes, which the
TPESampler can search more efficiently in a round of 50 trials.

The four surviving params are each narrowed to the convex hull of the top
trials with a modest buffer — enough for TPE to explore boundary behaviour
without re-visiting the failed regions (e.g. `top_k=20, chunk_overlap=0.5`
triggered a FAIL in trial 27).

Sampler and pruner are kept: TPE is well-suited for the lower-dim space,
and MedianPruner's 15% pruning rate was neither too aggressive nor too
slack.

## 4. Risks / things to watch next round

- Freezing the reranker / embedder locks out any late-round discovery that
  `bge-m3` embeddings work better for a specific query subset. If round 02
  plateaus below 0.70, round 03 should re-introduce `embed_model` as a
  categorical.
- Narrowing `chunk_overlap` to [0.1, 0.3] removes a 2-wide chunk of the
  range that had no top trials but only 7 COMPLETE trials total —
  importance of 0.03 is low-confidence. If round 02's best trial clusters
  near the new low boundary 0.1, widen back in round 03.
- The 6-of-32 low-temperature hits at exactly 0.0 may be an artifact of
  TPE's tendency to revisit the best point. Keep the lower bound at 0.0
  but watch for the opposite pattern (best trial at high=0.2 boundary).
